channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Britain's Princess of Wales says her cancer is in remission
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales said Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after she spent the day supporting other cancer patients at the hospital where she received treatment.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Austin Tex-Mex restaurant Trudy's to close only location still in operation
item.description: The restaurant, which started in 1977, wrote in an Instagram post that it was "saying goodbye" to the location on Burnet Road.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schnitker, NewsNation

item.title: Los Angeles couple's harrowing escape as Eaton Fire approached their home caught on video doorbell
item.description: A California couple's Ring video doorbell captured their harrowing escape as the Eaton Fire approached their Altadena home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: U.S. food inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the Jarratt, Virginia, factory that was shut down last year.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll reveals how much voters blame climate change, Newsom for California fire crisis
item.description: In the wake of several deadly California wildfires a new poll shows how Americans are reacting to the disaster, which left at least two dozen people dead and reduced entire neighborhoods to charred rubble.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Smith defends Trump prosecution as final Jan. 6 report released to the public
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith defended his investigations into President-elect Trump in a final report released publicly early Tuesday morning, saying his determinations were free from political interference and that, if permitted to proceed, his cases would have likely secured a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California faces new wildfire warnings as near hurricane-force winds regain strength
item.description: Southern California residents are facing dire new wildfire warnings and power shutoffs while they prepare to flee at a moment’s notice as fire-fueling winds blasted across the scarred landscape.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, Julie Watson And John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sexual predator sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah, planning to meet minor
item.description: A Florida man has been sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah and planning to have sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colorado apartment complex where armed gang members were seen on video will be closed
item.description: AURORA, Colo. (AP) — A Colorado apartment complex where armed members of a Venezuelan gang were caught on video entering a unit last summer is expected to close in about a month under an emergency court order, city officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Border Patrol reportedly captures Russian mercenary carrying drone, 2 passports and $4K
item.description: Texas border patrol agents arrested a Russian mercenary from Kazakhstan earlier this month, who admitted under questioning to being a member of the Wagner Group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Historic Powerball winner reportedly loses Malibu home in Palisades Fire
item.description: After winning $2.04 billion on a Powerball ticket in 2022, Edwin Castro reportedly bought multiple Los Angeles-area homes.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Senate advances Laken Riley bill past second hurdle
item.description: The Laken Riley Act on Monday cleared a second procedural hurdle in the Senate as talk turns to an amendment push by Democrats in order to potentially make alterations to the bill. Senators voted 82-10 on the motion to proceed to the legislation, allowing the chamber to begin debate.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: FBI, DHS warn of possible copycat attacks after NOLA ISIS-inspired vehicle attack
item.description: The FBI and DHS issued a public service announcement on Monday urging law enforcement agencies to be hyper aware of possible copycat ISIS-inspire attacks like in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fired Disney World employee pleads guilty to hacking menu, removing life-saving allergy information
item.description: A former Disney World employee changed menus to falsely indicate that food items didn't contain deadly allergens like peanuts, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More firefighters rush to Los Angeles ahead of stronger winds
item.description: A growing force of firefighters and equipment moved into the Los Angeles area as another round of powerful winds threatened to trigger new wildfires
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Magnitude 6.9 earthquake rattles southwestern Japan
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A strong earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 6.9 hit southwestern Japan on Monday, triggering public warnings to stay away from coastal areas because of a tsunami advisory, which was later called off. There were no immediate reports of damage.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New rule could slash nicotine levels in tobacco products
item.description: The Biden administration is poised to try to lower the amount of nicotine in tobacco products, an eleventh-hour effort that’s been years in the making.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: Jayden Daniels leads Commanders to first playoff win in nearly 7,000 days, 23-20 over the Buccaneers
item.description:
New owners. New general manager. New coach. New franchise quarterback.
That’s the formula — with an emphasis on the QB — that Washington rode to playoff success after nearly two decades of futility.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurts tosses 2 TD passes and Goedert throws 3 stiff-arms to lead Eagles past Packers 22-10
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Dallas Goedert fired one stiff arm to a Packers defender, smacked him again and then — in a sports city known for its Broad Street Bullies — dispatched one final shot to the face on his way to end zone.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lamar Jackson and Josh Allen will face off for a berth in the AFC championship game
item.description: Lamar Jackson vs. Josh Allen won’t decide the NFL MVP next week. The stakes are higher with a spot in the AFC championship game on the line.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clippers, Lakers to resume their wildfire-delayed schedules on Monday
item.description: NBA games are scheduled to return to wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles on Monday night, with the Clippers planning to resume their schedule when they host the Miami Heat and the Lakers set to host Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fires burn Los Angeles schools and destroy outdoor education sanctuaries
item.description: For Irina Contreras, a program manager for Los Angeles County’s Department of Arts and Culture, outdoor education was a refuge for both her and her daughter during the pandemic. Now, much of that refuge has been burned in the raging wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SHARON LURYE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: 16 dead, more than a dozen missing as crews continue fighting blazes
item.description: More than a dozen people have died as a result of the still-spreading wildfires burning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 16 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: California man says two of his cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu
item.description: A California man whose two cats died after drinking raw milk recalled for bird flu risk says he meant to keep his beloved pets healthy, but his efforts tragically backfired.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died
item.description: Bill McCartney, who coached Colorado to its only football national championship in 1990, has died. He was 84.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5
